operating_state: Total revenue increased 16% to $42.3 billion driven by advertising revenue growth to $41.4 billion, with ad impressions up 5% and average price per ad up 10%. Family of Apps revenue reached $41.9 billion while Reality Labs declined 6% due to lower Meta Quest sales. Growth was broadly positive across regions with particular strength in developed markets. Operating cash flows increased to $24.0 billion from $19.3 billion reflecting higher revenue collection.
cost_structure: Total costs increased 9% to $24.8 billion with research and development rising 22% primarily from higher employee compensation and AI infrastructure costs. Cost of revenue increased 14% mainly from data center operational expenses while general and administrative expenses decreased 34% due to lower legal costs. Operating margin expanded to 41% from 38% as income from operations grew to $17.6 billion. Extended server and network asset useful lives to 5.5 years reduced depreciation expense.
material_events: The company repurchased $12.8 billion of Class A common stock and paid $1.3 billion in dividends at an increased quarterly rate of $0.525 per share. Capital expenditures surged to $12.9 billion from $6.4 billion for data center construction and AI infrastructure. Multiple executive trading plans were established and the Compensation Committee increased target bonus percentages for named officers. Headcount increased 11% to 76,834 employees.
macro_exposures: European regulatory enforcement intensified with fines totaling approximately EUR €2 billion for Facebook Marketplace practices, subscription model non-compliance, and data transfer violations under appeal. Apple's iOS changes and platform restrictions continue negatively impacting advertiser targeting capabilities. Economic uncertainty including inflation and high interest rates affects marketer spending decisions.
forward_outlook: Management projects 2025 capital expenditures of $64-72 billion supporting core business and generative AI efforts, with Reality Labs operating losses expected to increase from continued metaverse investments. The company maintains contractual commitments primarily for infrastructure. Management expects modifications to European subscription model may significantly impact European business as early as Q3 2025 based on regulatory feedback.
uncertainty_profile: The FTC administrative proceeding seeking modifications to the 2020 consent order begins trial April 2025, potentially prohibiting minors' data use and limiting product launches. Multiple high-stakes proceedings include youth social media addiction cases with first trial November 2025, copyright claims related to AI training, and IRS transfer pricing disputes potentially resulting in $9.0 billion additional tax liability. The company faces potential structural remedies from competition authorities and uncertain tax positions totaling $10.9 billion.
